title: Candidate pairwise baseline-anchored residual scorer

queries: residual learning initialize residual branch to zero ranking model
learning to rank residual scorer preserve baseline candidate scoring

evidence.why_now: EXP108 preserved the salt-side autocomplete ranking signal near the strict baseline, while the later non-autoregressive scorer-replacement line repeatedly collapsed item-incoterms to the same MRR plateau. EXP129, EXP141, EXP150, and EXP156 all improved or moved Amazon-side controls but regressed item-incoterms, so the next justified mechanism is not another stronger replacement head; it is a baseline-preserving residual that can use candidate-aware interaction only as a small correction.

hypothesis: If the failed scalable pairwise and token-stat families are damaging the already-useful autocomplete baseline by replacing the score surface, then anchoring the multiclass decision to the raw-label baseline scorer and adding only a zero-initialized, tanh-bounded candidate-aware residual should preserve salt-side MRR while still allowing real candidate interaction to help when it is robust. This remains finetune-only, non-autoregressive, candidate-aware, and Stage 4-compatible because the scorer is O(C) after one prompt encoding and does not use exhaustive candidate rollout.
notes: Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not introduce pretrain, loss-family changes, sampling changes, or packaging changes.
This candidate intentionally preserves the raw-label baseline scorer as the primary multiclass decision surface and tests only a small candidate-aware residual.
The residual scorer is zero-initialized in the final affine layer, so the first optimization step starts from the baseline scoring behavior rather than a replacement head.
Use packed_batch_equivalent scheduling to improve GPU utilization while preserving effective global batch size for each representative task.
This is not another fixed pooled-label or token-stat weighting tweak; it changes the failure handling strategy from scorer replacement to baseline-anchored residual learning.
Completed cleanly on 2026-05-09 with global verdict: failed.
Result under replay-aware effective deltas: user-churn better, user-ltv neutral, item-incoterms worse.

item-incoterms did avoid the exact repeated 0.6785764663938492 plateau, landing at mrr=0.6990761110634157, but it remained far below the strict baseline mrr=0.8147880873466811 and outside the effective delta threshold.
Conclusion: anchoring to the raw-label baseline scorer partially softened the scorer-replacement collapse but did not preserve the baseline score surface enough to keep the salt-side task neutral.

Do not manually increase autocomplete_residual_scale as a next step; treat pairwise residuals over the current feature path as exhausted unless a new paper- or ablation-backed mechanism changes the information path.
